title: Betnet Performance Benchmarking
text: workflow evaluates accuracy betnet Julia package simulating full genomic temporal dataset. Unlike TransPhylo workflow, simulation explicitly models DNA sequence evolution using seq-gen.

what: Step 1: Data Generation and Julia Inference
title: Betnet Performance Benchmarking
text: simulate outbreak generate 1Mb genome individual. script calls betnet2.0.jl CLI perform Bayesian inference.
code: numsim <- 10 set.seed(42)  for (i in 1:numsim) {     # 1. Simulate Outbreak Truth     outbreak <- simulate_outbreak(infection_rate = 3.0, removal_rate = 3.0, target_size = 10)     write.csv(outbreak, paste0("outbreak", i, ".csv"))     numcase <- nrow(outbreak)      # 2. Extract Temporal Data (Infection and Removal times)     tempfile <- paste0("sim_temporal", i, ".csv")     write.csv(outbreak[, c(1, 5, 7)], file = tempfile, row.names = FALSE)      # 3. Simulate Genomic Sequence (DNA)     timetree <- build_timetree(outbreak, plot = FALSE)     phytree <- sim_phytree(timetree, murate = 10^-6)     ape::write.tree(phytree, "tree.tre")          seqfile <- paste0("seq", i)     # Using seq-gen for HKY model evolution across a 1Mb genome     system(paste("seq-gen -mHKY -l1000000 tree.tre >", seqfile))      # 4. Calculate SNP Distance Matrix     dna_data <- ape::read.dna(seqfile)     dist_mat <- ape::dist.dna(dna_data, model = "N", as.matrix = TRUE)          # Ensure indices match the temporal data     idx <- order(as.numeric(row.names(dist_mat)))     snp_mat <- dist_mat[idx, idx]     snpfile <- paste0("sim_snp", i, ".csv")     write.csv(snp_mat, file = snpfile, row.names = FALSE)          # 5. Execute betnet Inference via Julia CLI     betnet_cmd <- paste0("julia betnet2.0.jl -t ", tempfile, " -s ", snpfile, " -o parameter", i, ".csv")     system(betnet_cmd) }

title: Betnet Performance Benchmarking
text: Julia MCMC chains complete, read posterior samples back R using summary_betnet compare inferred infectors simulation truth.
code: betnet_accuracy <- numeric(numsim)  for (i in 1:numsim) {     # Load Truth and Estimation     outbreak_true <- read.csv(paste0("outbreak", i, ".csv"))[, -1]     input_mcmc <- paste0("parameter", i, ".csv")          # Extract the MAP (Maximum A Posteriori) transmission network     outbreak_est <- summary_betnet(input_mcmc,                                     onsite = outbreak_true$onsite_time,                                     removal_time = outbreak_true$removal_time,                                     plot = FALSE)$transmission          # Calculate difference (0 indicates correct infector identification)     diff_table <- outbreak_est - outbreak_true     betnet_accuracy[i] <- sum(diff_table$infector == 0) / (nrow(outbreak_true) - 1) }  cat("Mean betnet Accuracy:", mean(betnet_accuracy))

code: @Manual{,   title = {transNetwork: Transmission Network Reconstruction and Simulation},   author = {Liang Liu and Jihyun Kim},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0, https://lliu1871.github.io/transNetwork},   url = {https://github.com/lliu1871/transNetwork}, }

title: Transmission Network Reconstruction and Simulation
text: goal transNetwork provide unified framework simulating, reconstructing, visualizing infectious disease transmission networks using genomic epidemiological data.

title: Transmission Network Reconstruction and Simulation
text: Simulation: Generate stochastic outbreaks custom latent infectious periods. Reconstruction: Build transmission trees MCMC output (BetNet). Phylogenetics: Convert transmission histories phylogenetic trees. Metrics: Calculate pairwise transmission distances Recent Common Infectors (MRCI). Visualization: High-level plotting functions networks, timelines, trees.

title: Transmission Network Reconstruction and Simulation
text: can install development version transNetwork GitHub : Note: automatically install necessary dependencies, including ape, igraph, phybase.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("lliu1871/transNetwork")

title: Transmission Network Reconstruction and Simulation
text: basic example simulate outbreak visualize transmission network:
code: library(transNetwork) library(TransPhylo)  # 1. Simulate an outbreak of 50 individuals outbreak_data <- simulate_outbreak(target_size = 50, infection_rate = 1.5)  # 2. Build a time tree from the transmission distances time_tree <- build_timetree(outbreak_data, plot = TRUE)  # 3. TransPhylo analysis ptree <- ptreeFromPhylo(time_tree, dateLastSample = 2007.964) res <- inferTTree(ptree, mcmcIterations = 10000, w.shape = 10, w.scale = 0.1, dateT = 2008)  # 4. Plot the estimated transmission network matrixwiw <- computeMatWIW(res, burnin = 0.5) netedges <- transmission_edges_matrixwiw(matrixwiw) transplot(netedges, style = 1)

title: Transmission Network Reconstruction and Simulation
text: package developed academic researchers evolutionary genomics phylogenetics. specifically addresses “coalescent lag” transmission events genetic divergence, allowing accurate benchmarking molecular clock models outbreak settings.

what: Add Coalescent Lag to Transmission Distances — add_coalescent_distance
title: Add Coalescent Lag to Transmission Distances — add_coalescent_distance
text: Augments transmission distance matrix stochastic coalescent distances simulate divergence transmission tree gene tree.

title: Add Coalescent Lag to Transmission Distances — add_coalescent_distance
code: add_coalescent_distance(pairwise_dist, theta)

title: Add Coalescent Lag to Transmission Distances — add_coalescent_distance
text: pairwise_dist symmetric numeric matrix transmission distances. theta Numeric. population size parameter (mean coalescent distance).

title: Add Coalescent Lag to Transmission Distances — add_coalescent_distance
text: symmetric numeric matrix entry $(, j)$ sum transmission distance simulated coalescent lag.

what: Build a Time Tree via Neighbor-Joining — build_timetree
title: Build a Time Tree via Neighbor-Joining — build_timetree
text: Constructs phylogenetic time tree outbreak matrix calculating pairwise transmission distances applying Neighbor-Joining algorithm.

title: Build a Time Tree via Neighbor-Joining — build_timetree
code: build_timetree(outbreak, root = 1, plot = FALSE, ...)

title: Build a Time Tree via Neighbor-Joining — build_timetree
text: outbreak data frame 'id', 'parent_id', 'infection_time', 'removal_time'. root Integer Character. ID node use root (usually index case). plot Logical. TRUE, plots resulting phylogeny. ... Additional arguments passed ape::plot.phylo.

title: Build a Time Tree via Neighbor-Joining — build_timetree
text: object class phylo (invisibly).

what: Compute All Pairwise Transmission Distances — find_all_pairwise_distances
title: Compute All Pairwise Transmission Distances — find_all_pairwise_distances
text: Calculates temporal distance every pair individuals outbreak based Recent Common Infector (MRCI).

title: Compute All Pairwise Transmission Distances — find_all_pairwise_distances
code: find_all_pairwise_distances(outbreak, return_matrix = TRUE)

title: Compute All Pairwise Transmission Distances — find_all_pairwise_distances
text: outbreak data frame 'infectee', 'infector', 'infection_time', 'removal_time'. return_matrix Logical. TRUE (default), returns symmetric $nxn$ numeric matrix. FALSE, returns long-format data frame.

title: Compute All Pairwise Transmission Distances — find_all_pairwise_distances
text: return_matrix TRUE, symmetric matrix. Otherwise, data.frame containing columns IDs, shared infector, infector's infection time, calculated distance.

title: Trace Infection Ancestry — find_infectors
text: Traverses transmission chain upwards specific individual identify direct indirect infectors (ancestors).

title: Trace Infection Ancestry — find_infectors
code: find_infectors(outbreak, id, include_self = TRUE)

title: Trace Infection Ancestry — find_infectors
text: outbreak data frame containing least id parent_id. id Integer. ID individual trace. include_self Logical. TRUE, returned vector starts id.

title: Trace Infection Ancestry — find_infectors
text: integer vector IDs representing ancestry chain, ordered individual/parent index case (root).

what: Find the Most Recent Common Infector (MRCI) — find_mrci
title: Find the Most Recent Common Infector (MRCI) — find_mrci
text: Identifies shared infector two individuals outbreak calculates transmission distance .

title: Find the Most Recent Common Infector (MRCI) — find_mrci
code: find_mrci(outbreak, id1, id2, include_self = TRUE)

title: Find the Most Recent Common Infector (MRCI) — find_mrci
text: outbreak data frame containing 'id', 'parent_id', 'infection_time', 'removal_time'. id1 Integer/Character ID first individual. id2 Integer/Character ID second individual. include_self Logical. TRUE, search common infector includes individuals .

title: Find the Most Recent Common Infector (MRCI) — find_mrci
text: list containing: mrci: ID recent common infector (NA shared infector exists). distance: calculated temporal distance two individuals.

what: Simulate a Phylogenetic Tree from a Time Tree — sim_phytree
title: Simulate a Phylogenetic Tree from a Time Tree — sim_phytree
text: Converts epidemiological time tree phylogenetic tree scaling branch lengths mutation rate optionally simulating coalescent process using multispecies coalescent framework.

title: Simulate a Phylogenetic Tree from a Time Tree — sim_phytree
code: sim_phytree(timetree, murate = 0, theta = 0)

title: Simulate a Phylogenetic Tree from a Time Tree — sim_phytree
text: timetree object class phylo (ape package). murate Numeric. Mutation rate used scale time tree branch lengths. theta Numeric. Population size parameter coalescent simulation. 0, simple scaling performed.

title: Simulate a Phylogenetic Tree from a Time Tree — sim_phytree
text: object class phylo representing simulated gene tree.

title: Simulate a Stochastic Outbreak — simulate_outbreak
text: Simulates infectious disease outbreak using branching process. individual latent period, onsite time, infectious period followed removal.

title: Simulate a Stochastic Outbreak — simulate_outbreak
code: simulate_outbreak(   initial_infection_time = 0,   infection_rate = 2,   removal_rate = 2,   latent_mean = 0.2,   target_size = 50 )

title: Simulate a Stochastic Outbreak — simulate_outbreak
text: initial_infection_time Numeric. time first case infected (default 0). infection_rate Numeric. rate secondary infections per unit time. removal_rate Numeric. rate removal infectious state. latent_mean Numeric. degrees freedom chi-square distribution latent period. target_size Integer. minimum number cases required successful simulation.

title: Simulate a Stochastic Outbreak — simulate_outbreak
text: data frame (outbreak matrix) target_size rows columns IDs, times (infection, onsite, removal), periods (latent, infectious).

what: Summary and Parameter Estimation for BetNet Output — summary_betnet
title: Summary and Parameter Estimation for BetNet Output — summary_betnet
text: Processes MCMC output BetNet, generates convergence plots, calculates posterior means parameters, reconstructs likely transmission network.

title: Summary and Parameter Estimation for BetNet Output — summary_betnet
code: summary_betnet(   betnet_output_file,   burnin = 0.1,   onsite_time,   removal_time,   plot = TRUE )

title: Summary and Parameter Estimation for BetNet Output — summary_betnet
text: betnet_output_file String. Path CSV output BetNet. burnin Numeric. Proportion initial iterations discard (default 0.1). onsite_time Numeric vector. Onsite times cases outbreak. removal_time Numeric vector. Removal times cases outbreak. plot Logical. TRUE, displays MCMC trace plots.

title: Summary and Parameter Estimation for BetNet Output — summary_betnet
text: list containing: theta: Posterior mean theta parameter. mu: Posterior mean mutation rate. infrate: Posterior mean infection rate. transmission: data frame reconstructed transmission network.

what: Convert Who-Infected-Who Matrix to Edge List — transmission_edges_matrixwiw
title: Convert Who-Infected-Who Matrix to Edge List — transmission_edges_matrixwiw
text: Takes posterior probability matrix identifies likely infector case (infectee) based highest posterior probability.

title: Convert Who-Infected-Who Matrix to Edge List — transmission_edges_matrixwiw
code: transmission_edges_matrixwiw(matrix_wiw)

title: Convert Who-Infected-Who Matrix to Edge List — transmission_edges_matrixwiw
text: matrix_wiw numeric matrix matrix_wiw[, j] probability individual infected individual j. Rows represent potential infectors columns represent infectees.

title: Convert Who-Infected-Who Matrix to Edge List — transmission_edges_matrixwiw
text: data frame three columns: infectee: Character ID case. infector: Character ID likely infector (NA unknown/zero prob). prob: posterior probability associated edge.

title: Plot Transmission Networks — transplot
text: Visualizes transmission matrix network graph, rooted tree, timeline, depending chosen style.

title: Plot Transmission Networks — transplot
code: transplot(   transmission,   style = 1,   vcolor = "lightblue",   vsize = 12,   label.cex = 1.5,   showlabel = FALSE,   dateLastSample = 2008 )

title: Plot Transmission Networks — transplot
text: transmission data frame representing transmission matrix. Must contain 'infector', 'infectee', 'prob'. style Integer. 1 standard network, 2 rooted tree, 3 timeline, 4 detailed transmission plot. vcolor node color. vsize node size. label.cex node label scaling. showlabel Logical. TRUE, displays probability weights edges. dateLastSample Numeric. reference year/date timeline styles.

title: Plot Transmission Networks — transplot
text: function primarily produces plot active graphics device. returns NULL invisibly.

what: Convert Outbreak Data to a ttree Object — ttree_from_transmission
title: Convert Outbreak Data to a ttree Object — ttree_from_transmission
text: Transforms outbreak data frame structured ttree list suitable transmission tree plotting.

title: Convert Outbreak Data to a ttree Object — ttree_from_transmission
code: ttree_from_transmission(outbreak, dateLastSample)

title: Convert Outbreak Data to a ttree Object — ttree_from_transmission
text: outbreak data frame containing least: removal_time, infectious_period, latent_period, parent_id, id. dateLastSample Numeric. calendar date (e.g., 2024.5) corresponding maximum removal time dataset.

title: Convert Outbreak Data to a ttree Object — ttree_from_transmission
text: list class ttree containing: ttree: matrix columns infection time, removal time, parent ID. nam: character vector individual IDs.
